verses.1: The wicked flee when no one is pursuing, But the righteous are bold as a lion.
verses.2: Due to a wrongdoing of a land its leaders are many, But by a person of understanding and knowledge, so it endures.
verses.3: A poor man who oppresses the helpless Is like a driving rain which leaves no food.
verses.4: Those who abandon the Law praise the wicked, But those who keep the Law strive against them.
verses.5: Evil people do not understand justice, But those who seek the LORD understand everything.
verses.6: Better is a poor person who walks in his integrity, Than a person who is crooked, though he is rich.
verses.7: He who keeps the Law is a discerning son, But he who is a companion of gluttons humiliates his father.
verses.8: One who increases his wealth by interest of any kind, Collects it for one who is gracious to the poor.
verses.9: One who turns his ear away from listening to the Law, Even his prayer is an abomination.
verses.10: One who leads the upright astray in an evil way Will himself fall into his own pit, But the blameless will inherit good.
verses.11: The rich person is wise in his own eyes, But the poor who has understanding sees through him.
verses.12: When the righteous triumph, there is great glory, But when the wicked rise, people hide themselves.
verses.13: One who conceals his wrongdoings will not prosper, But one who confesses and abandons them will find compassion.
verses.14: How blessed is the person who fears always, But one who hardens his heart will fall into disaster.
verses.15: Like a roaring lion and a rushing bear Is a wicked ruler over a poor people.
verses.16: A leader who is a great oppressor lacks understanding, But a person who hates unjust gain will prolong his days.
verses.17: A person who is burdened with the guilt of human blood Will be a fugitive until death; no one is to support him!
verses.18: One who walks blamelessly will receive help, But one who is crooked will fall all at once.
verses.19: One who works his land will have plenty of food, But one who follows empty pursuits will have plenty of poverty.
verses.20: A faithful person will abound with blessings, But one who hurries to be rich will not go unpunished.
verses.21: To show partiality is not good, Because for a piece of bread a man will do wrong.
verses.22: A person with an evil eye hurries after wealth And does not know that poverty will come upon him.
verses.23: One who rebukes a person will afterward find more favor Than one who flatters with the tongue.
verses.24: He who robs his father or his mother And says, “There is no wrong done,” Is the companion of a person who destroys.
verses.25: An arrogant person stirs up strife, But one who trusts in the LORD will prosper.
verses.26: One who trusts in his own heart is a fool, But one who walks wisely will flee to safety.
verses.27: One who gives to the poor will never lack anything, But one who shuts his eyes will have many curses.
verses.28: When the wicked rise, people hide themselves; But when they perish, the righteous increase.
